channel.title: AI Builder's Field Guide — Recently Added
channel.description: A daily-curated wiki of the tools, models, and patterns that AI power-users and builders actually reach for.

item.title: Velo
item.description: Velo is an AI video creation platform that transforms raw screen recordings into polished, shareable video messages with automated scripts and AI avatars. — It simplifies video production for demos and explainers by automating scriptwriting and avatar generation, reducing the need for extensive editing compared to traditional video tools.

item.title: Kilo Code
item.description: Kilo Code is an open-source AI coding agent for IDEs and CLI that generates code, automates tasks, and debugs across over 500 models. — It offers an open and flexible alternative to proprietary AI coding assistants by supporting a wide range of models and providing deep integration into developer workflows.

item.title: OpenClaw
item.description: OpenClaw is a free and open-source personal AI assistant that runs locally on users' devices, connecting AI models to numerous integrations. — It offers a privacy-focused, local-first approach to AI assistance with extensive integration capabilities and self-extending skill development, contrasting with cloud-dependent alternatives.

item.title: Mistral Vibe
item.description: Mistral Vibe is a unified AI agent platform for work and coding, designed for long-running, multi-step tasks across various environments. — It integrates diverse productivity and coding functions into a single agent, enabling comprehensive automation of complex workflows beyond simple conversational AI.

item.title: OpenCode
item.description: OpenCode is an open-source, terminal-based AI coding agent that integrates with Git for commit-level code edits and supports over 100 languages. — Unlike commercial AI coding assistants, OpenCode offers a free, self-hosted option with broad language support and deep codebase context for developers.

item.title: RAMPART (Microsoft)
item.description: RAMPART is a Pytest-native, open-source framework from Microsoft for testing the safety and security of AI agents during development. — It provides a structured way to red-team AI agents early in the development lifecycle, complementing existing observability tools by focusing on security vulnerabilities.

item.title: Google Antigravity
item.description: An agent-first development platform and IDE by Google for building and orchestrating autonomous AI agents across editor, terminal, and browser. — It shifts from traditional AI code assistance to a system where AI agents operate with greater autonomy, providing an abstracted UI for managing parallel subagents and scheduled tasks.

item.title: CodeWhale
item.description: CodeWhale is a terminal-based AI coding agent designed for DeepSeek models, engineered for prefix-cache stability. — It provides a specialized coding agent optimized for DeepSeek models, offering a stable terminal experience for developers.
